title: Containers refresh
body:
Add a new command to manually reset container state, allowing us to more gracefully handle potentially-breaking changes in the database. This would run as a post-install script in package managers, and perform a task somewhat similar to a daemon restart (except without the daemon) - containers go down, have their state updated to reflect changes in the current version, and come back up.

Also make some changes to the way we handle CGroups to address bugs I found while writing this.

comments.body: This is WIP pending tests and manpages, but I wanted to get the code out for review

I would prefer to make this a subcommand of podman containers

`# podman containers refresh`

I am currently working on 

`# podman containers cleanup`
Manpages and bash completions in, working on tests
Tests are in, removing WIP label

Tests are green, I'd like to get this in before cutting a release
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat @baude @umohnani8  PTAL

LGTM ... though I have been out for a while.  If I had a vote, I would prefer something like podman --refresh rather than an actual subcommand that will only be used once in a while.  But I might be too late to the party for that.

Merging so we can cut a release
@rh-atomic-bot r=baude
